text: On producing the waves of frequency 1000 Hz in a Kundt's tube, the total distance between 6 successive nodes is 85 cm. Then, the speed of sound in the gas filled in the tube is
explanation: Frequency \(f = 1000\) Hz. Total distance between 6 successive nodes is 0.85 m. Distance between 6 successive nodes = \(\frac{5\lambda}{2}\). So, \(\frac{5\lambda}{2} = 0.85\) ⇒ \(\lambda = 0.34\) m. Speed of sound \(v = f \lambda = 1000 \times 0.34 = 340\) ms⁻¹.
name: Questions Item 1

text: Two tuning forks X and Y are of frequencies 280 Hz and 284 Hz. A third tuning fork Z is of unknown frequency. When X and Z are sounded together certain beats are heard per second. When Y and Z are sounded together beat frequency is found to be thrice as great. The frequency of Z is
explanation: Let beat frequency with X be \(b\), then with Y it is \(3b\). So, \(|n_Z - 280| = b\) and \(|n_Z - 284| = 3b\). Solving equations \(n_Z - 280 = b\) and \(n_Z - 284 = 3b\) (assuming \(n_Z\) between them) gives \(n_Z = 278\) Hz.
name: Questions Item 2

text: What is the phase difference between two particles 25 m apart in a wave represented by equation \(y = 0.03 \sin(\pi [2t - 0.01x])\) travelling in a medium?
explanation: Compare with \(y = A \sin(\omega t - kx)\), \(k = 0.01 \pi\). \(\lambda = 2 / 0.01 = 200\) m. Phase difference \(\Delta \phi = \frac{2\pi}{\lambda} \times \Delta x = \frac{2\pi}{200} \times 25 = \pi/4\).
name: Questions Item 3

text: Two waves of frequency \(f\) and amplitude \(a\) superimpose with each other. The total intensity is directly proportional to
explanation: Resultant amplitude \(A = a + a = 2a\). Intensity \(I \propto A^2\), so \(I \propto (2a)^2 = 4a^2\).
name: Questions Item 4

text: On getting reflected at a surface, the intensity of sound is found to be decreased by 20%. If \(A\) be the amplitude of the incident sound waves, then the amplitude of reflected sound waves is
explanation: Reflected intensity \(I_r = 0.8 I_i\). Since \(I \propto A^2\), \(\frac{I_r}{I_i} = \left(\frac{A_r}{A}\right)^2 = 0.8\). So \(A_r = A \sqrt{0.8} = \frac{2A}{\sqrt{5}}\).
name: Questions Item 5

text: The distance between the successive node and anti-node is
explanation: In a standing wave, distance between a node and the adjacent anti-node is always \(\lambda/4\).
name: Questions Item 6

text: A sound source is moving towards a stationary listener with 1/10th of the speed of sound. The ratio of apparent to real frequency is
explanation: Apparent frequency \(f' = \frac{v}{v - v_s} f = \frac{v}{v - v/10} f = \frac{10}{9} f\). Ratio = 10/9.
name: Questions Item 7

text: A piano wire with a diameter of 0.90 mm is replaced by another wire of diameter 0.93 mm of the same material. If tension of wire is kept the same, then the percentage change in frequency of fundamental tone is
explanation: Frequency \(f \propto 1/d\) (since \(m \propto d^2\)). Ratio \(f_2/f_1 = d_1/d_2 = 0.9/0.93\). Percentage change = \((0.9/0.93 - 1) \times 100 = -3.2\)%
name: Questions Item 8

text: When the area of cross-section of a stretched wire is halved and tension is doubled, the speed of propagation of transverse waves along it becomes k times the initial speed. Then, k
explanation: Speed \(v = \sqrt{T/m}\), \(m \propto A\). New \(v' = \sqrt{2T / (A/2)} = \sqrt{4} v = 2v\). Hence \(k=2\).
name: Questions Item 9

text: A closed organ pipe and an open organ pipe of same length produce 2 beats/s when they are set into vibrations together in fundamental mode. The length of open pipe is now halved and that of closed pipe is doubled. The number of beats produced will be
explanation: Initially \(f_o = 2 f_c\), difference 2 Hz gives \(f_c = 2\) Hz, \(f_o = 4\) Hz. After change, new open frequency 8 Hz, new closed 1 Hz. Beats = 7.
name: Questions Item 10

text: An observer moves towards a stationary source of sound with a speed 1/5th of the speed of sound. The wavelength and frequency of the waves emitted by the source are λ and f respectively. The apparent frequency and wavelength heard by the observer are respectively
explanation: Apparent frequency \(f' = (v + v_o)/v \times f = 1.2f\). Wavelength remains λ (observer motion does not change wavelength).
name: Questions Item 11

text: A siren placed at a railway platform is emitting a sound of frequency 5 kHz. A passenger sitting in a moving train A records the frequency of the siren as 5.5 kHz. During his return journey by train B he records the frequency of the siren as 6 kHz. The ratio of the speed of train B to that of train A is
explanation: Using Doppler formula for approach and recede, solving ratio of speeds gives 2.
name: Questions Item 12

text: Speed of a transverse wave travelling in a wire of length 50 cm, cross-sectional area 1 mm² and mass 5 g is 80 ms⁻¹. The Young's modulus of the material of the wire is 4 × 10¹¹ Nm⁻². The extension in the length of the wire is
explanation: From wave speed \(v = \sqrt{T/\mu}\), T calculated. Then extension \(\Delta l = T L / (A Y) = 8 \times 10^{-5}\) m.
name: Questions Item 13

text: At a temperature of 314 K and a pressure of 100 kPa, the speed of sound in a gas is 1380 ms⁻¹. The radius of each gas molecule is 0.5Å. The frequency of sound at which the wavelength of sound wave in the gas becomes equal to the mean free path of the gas molecules is
explanation: Mean free path \(\lambda = 1/(\sqrt{2} \pi d^2 n)\). Frequency when \(\lambda = v/f\), solving gives 1000√2 MHz.
name: Questions Item 14

text: At a temperature of 27°C, two identical organ pipes produce notes of frequency 140 Hz. If the temperature of one pipe is raised to 57.75°C, then the number of beats produced per second is
explanation: Frequency ∝ √T. New frequency ratio √(330.75/300) = 1.05. Beats = 140 × 0.05 = 7.
name: Questions Item 15

text: A source producing sound of frequency 720 Hz is falling freely from the top of a tower of height 20 m. The frequency of sound heard by an observer on the top of the tower when the source just reaches the ground is (g = 10 ms⁻², v = 340 ms⁻¹)
explanation: Velocity at ground √(2gh) = 20 m/s. Doppler \(f' = f \times v/(v + v_s) = 720 \times 340/360 = 680\) Hz.
name: Questions Item 16

text: Match the following List I with List II
explanation: Transverse: vibrations perpendicular. Longitudinal: parallel. Beats: superposition same direction. Stationary: opposite directions.
name: Questions Item 17

text: A police car moving at 22 ms⁻¹ chases a motor cyclist. The police man sounds horn at 176 Hz, while both of them move towards a stationary siren of frequency 165 Hz. If the number of beats heard by the motor cyclist per second is zero, then the speed of motorcycle is
explanation: Doppler from police horn and siren cancel beats at motorcycle speed 22 m/s.
name: Questions Item 18

text: A solid ball is suspended from the ceiling of a motor car through a light string. A transverse pulse travels at the speed 60 cm·s⁻¹ on the string, when the car is at rest. When the car accelerates on a horizontal road, then speed of the pulse is 66 cm·s⁻¹. The acceleration of the car is nearly
explanation: Tension increases to effective g. Ratio speeds give a ≈ 6.8 m/s².
name: Questions Item 19

text: A reflector is moving with 20 ms⁻¹ towards a stationary source of sound. If the source is producing sound waves of 160 Hz, then the wavelength of the reflected wave is
explanation: Apparent frequency for reflection gives wavelength 17/9 m.
name: Questions Item 20

text: An object of density 2000 kg·m⁻³ is hung from a thin light wire. The fundamental frequency of the transverse waves in the wire is 200 Hz. If the object is immersed in water such that half of its volume is submerged, then the fundamental frequency of the transverse waves in the wire is
explanation: Tension reduces to effective weight. New frequency √(3/4) × 200 ≈ 173.2 Hz.
name: Questions Item 21

text: An observer and a source emitting sound of frequency 120 Hz are on the X-axis. The observer is stationary while the source of sound is in motion given by the equation x = 3 sin ωt. If the difference between the maximum and minimum frequencies of the sound observed by the observer is 22 Hz, then the value of ω is
explanation: Maximum and minimum Doppler frequencies differ by 22 Hz. Solving gives ω = 10 rad/s.
name: Questions Item 22

text: If the length of a stretched string is shortened by x% and the tension is increased by 44%. Then the ratio of the final and initial fundamental frequencies is 1 : 2, then the value of x is
explanation: Frequency ratio leads to x = 40.
name: Questions Item 23

text: A small source of sound vibrating at a frequency 500 Hz is rotated along a circle of radius 100/π cm at a constant angular speed of 5 revolutions per second. The minimum and maximum frequency of the sound observed by a listener situation in the plane of the circle is
explanation: Source speed gives min and max Doppler frequencies 485.4 and 515.5 Hz.
name: Questions Item 24

text: A progressive wave of frequency 500 Hz is travelling with a velocity of 360 ms⁻¹. The distance between the two points, having a phase difference of 60° is
explanation: Phase difference 60° = π/3 rad = (2π/λ) Δx. λ = v/f = 0.72 m. Δx = λ/6 = 0.12 m.
name: Questions Item 25
